dimension_notes_summary.cross_module_architecture: Strictly layered backend: Domain/ is a pure leaf (avg_fan_out 0, avg_fan_in 21); every directory edge in the coupling_matrix flows downward (Features/Messages, Auth, Data, Rooms, Moderation -> Domain)
dimension_notes_summary.high_level_elegance: Backend uses a clean, domain-aligned decomposition: Domain/ (entities + small value helpers Slug/Text), Data/ (AppDbContext, DbSeeder, design-time factory), Auth/, Email/, Realtime/, Storage/, and Fea
dimension_notes_summary.convention_outlier: Backend C# is uniformly conventional: every controller (AuthController, MessagesController, RoomsController, ModerationController, PresenceController) uses primary constructors, [ApiController]/[Route
dimension_notes_summary.error_consistency: Service layer uses a uniform Result-record idiom for expected domain failures: MessageCreateResult, DeleteMessageResult, ReportMessageResult (MessageService.cs:187-205) and CreateRoomResult (RoomServi
dimension_notes_summary.naming_quality: Backend service/reader split is named by intent: MessageService holds commands (CreateMessageAsync, UpvoteAsync, SoftDeleteAsync, ReportAsync), MessageReader holds reads (ListRoomMessagesAsync, ListRe
dimension_notes_summary.abstraction_fitness: Only two I* interfaces exist (IImageStorage, IEmailSender) and both are genuine external-boundary adapters: IEmailSender is replaced with CapturingEmailSender in AuthFlowTests.cs (real test seam), and
dimension_notes_summary.dependency_health: Frontend (frontend/package.json) has 5 production deps and every one is used: chart.js (views/Moderation.tsx), lucide-react (7 files), @microsoft/signalr (src/realtime.ts), react and react-dom (src/ma
dimension_notes_summary.low_level_elegance: MessageService.CreateMessageAsync (MessageService.cs:15-97) reads as a clean linear pipeline: guard clauses for empty/too-long body, early-return validation for room/parent, scoped try/catch only arou
dimension_notes_summary.mid_level_elegance: Service layer returns explicit result records (MessageCreateResult, DeleteMessageResult, ReportMessageResult, CreateRoomResult) with static factory methods; controllers translate the single Error code
dimension_notes_summary.test_strategy: Integration suite (CoreApiTests, AuthFlowTests, RealtimeStorageModerationTests, SeedAdminTests, CorsTests, SmokeTests) runs against a real Postgres via Testcontainers + WebApplicationFactory<Program>,
dimension_notes_summary.api_surface_coherence: Controllers are highly uniform: every controller uses primary-constructor DI, [ApiController]+[Route("api/...")], ActionResult<T> return types, a trailing CancellationToken ct parameter, and [EnableRa
dimension_notes_summary.authorization_consistency: Permission strings are fully centralized in the Roles static class (Domain/Enums.cs) and used everywhere: [Authorize(Roles = Roles.Admin)] (RoomsController.cs:27, ModerationController.cs:233), [Author
dimension_notes_summary.ai_generated_debt: Codebase comment ratio is 0.022 (very low); every comment sampled explains WHY, not WHAT — e.g. MessageService.cs:64-65 explains reparenting to bound tree depth, TokenService.cs:46-60 explains replay-
dimension_notes_summary.incomplete_migration: Frontend has a single HTTP client: one fetch wrapper in frontend/src/api.ts (dispatch/send), no axios+fetch coexistence; no moment/dayjs/date-fns dual date libs; all components functional (no class+fu
dimension_notes_summary.package_organization: Backend server/Sheshi.Api/ uses clean feature/concern folders (Auth/, Data/, Domain/, Email/, Features/{Messages,Moderation,Rooms}/, Realtime/, Storage/) where namespaces match directories one-to-one
dimension_notes_summary.initialization_coupling: Program.cs is a clean ASP.NET Core minimal-host bootstrap: all cross-component wiring (TokenService, MessageService, RoomService, HighlightsService, IImageStorage, RealtimeNotifier, PresenceTracker) i
dimension_notes_summary.design_coherence: Backend uses one uniform per-feature shape: Controller (HTTP/auth mapping) + Service (commands) + Reader (queries) + DTOs, repeated across Messages, Rooms, Moderation, Auth without divergence.
dimension_notes_summary.contract_coherence: DTO/TS parity is exact and disciplined: every C# record DTO (MessageDto, RoomDto, UserDto, AuthResponse, ModReportDto, ModUserDto, ModAnalyticsDto tree, CursorPageDto<T>) maps field-for-field to the s
dimension_notes_summary.logic_clarity: HighlightsService.LoadStatsAsync (server/Sheshi.Api/Features/Messages/HighlightsService.cs:175-191) walks parent chains with a `seen` HashSet cycle guard and bounded TryGetValue lookups; control flow
dimension_notes_summary.type_safety: C# project sets <Nullable>enable</Nullable> (Sheshi.Api.csproj:5) and frontend tsconfig.app.json sets strict:true — strong baseline enforced by the compiler, not convention.

review_issue_summaries.summary: ModerationController holds all analytics/aggregation logic inline while sibling features use a controller+service split
codebase_analysis.md Directory Structure and Gaps sections no longer match runtime reality
Empty leftover Controllers/ directory implies a flat-controllers layout the codebase abandoned for feature folders
views/feeds.tsx is lowercase while sibling view files Auth.tsx/Moderation.tsx/Profile.tsx are PascalCase
Backend ASP.NET Core 10.0.x packages pinned at inconsistent patch levels (10.0.0/10.0.2/10.0.7/10.0.8)
TryParseStatus computes parsed via switch then re-derives success from a parallel three-literal check, evaluating ToLowerInvariant three times
Service result error codes cross the service->controller seam as bare strings matched by literal comparison
RealtimeNotifier.MessageChangedAsync(Guid, Guid?, ...) overload has no callers
Frontend hotScore duplicates backend HighlightsService recency constants across the C#/TS boundary
React/TS frontend SPA has no automated tests and no configured test runner
Pagination ordering test depends on a 5ms Task.Delay to separate CreatedAt timestamps
RealtimeNotifier.MessageChangedAsync(Guid roomId, Guid? threadId, ct) overload has zero callers
Sibling write endpoints inconsistently apply the IsBanned guard
ChatHub has no [Authorize] despite JWT-from-query wiring for /hub
Privileged moderation/admin actions leave no audit trail
Empty Controllers/ directory contradicts the feature-folder controller convention
Domain/ co-locates static text utilities (Slug.cs, Text.cs) with persistence entities
SignalR hub payloads use camelCase while REST uses snake_case for the same DTOs; frontend reads both casings defensively
TryParseStatus re-runs the lowercased string-set check for its return instead of using the switch it computed
Redundant non-null assertions on props.thread after an early-return guard already narrows it
ReportStatus enum re-parsed from raw query string with an awkward double-lowercase switch and redundant boolean check

dimension_notes_summary.high_level_elegance: Backend top-level folders map cleanly to capabilities: Domain/ (entities), Data/ (DbContext+seeder), Auth/, Common/ (Text+Slug helpers), Email/, Realtime/ (SignalR), Storage/ (image storage), and Feat
dimension_notes_summary.convention_outlier: Backend Features/ directories follow a uniform vertical-slice convention: each has XController.cs, XService.cs, XDtos.cs with PascalCase filenames, records for DTOs, primary-constructor classes, and f

dimension_notes_summary.api_surface_coherence: Every controller follows one shape: primary-constructor ControllerBase with [ApiController], [Route("api/...")], [EnableRateLimiting(...)], returning ActionResult<T>/IActionResult. A developer can pre
dimension_notes_summary.authorization_consistency: Role/permission strings are centralized as constants in Domain/Enums.cs (Roles.User/Moderator/Admin/ModeratorOrAdmin); every controller references Roles.* — no magic permission literals anywhere (Room
dimension_notes_summary.package_organization: Backend uses a clean feature-folder layout: Auth/, Common/, Data/, Domain/, Email/, Features/{Messages,Moderation,Rooms}/, Realtime/, Storage/ — each directory name maps directly to its contained resp
dimension_notes_summary.contract_coherence: Result-style record contracts are consistent and honest: MessageCreateResult/DeleteMessageResult/ReportMessageResult (MessageService.cs:185-203), ModActionResult (ModerationService.cs:270-276), and Cr

review_issue_summaries.summary: appSupport.ts bundles unrelated concern families under a generic 'support' name, blurring file ownership
components/ uses lowercase filenames while sibling views/ uses PascalCase for the same React component module type
The 'effective root id' derivation is duplicated across three message-feature files instead of one shared helper
RealtimeNotifier emits a structured 'message_changed' payload no client or test consumes
